titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Vladimir Horowitz Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by staff of Gilmore Music Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday August 8, 2018 at 20:00

did.physdesc: (56 containers)

did.unittitle: Additions from Vladimir Horowitz and Wanda Toscanini Horowitz
did.physdesc: (52 containers)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries is divided into two categories: family correspondence and general correspondence.
The family correspondence incluldes letters to and from Vladimir, Wanda and Sonia, as well as Wanda's mother, Carla DeMartini, and her father, Arturo Toscanini.

The general correspondence consists chiefly of fan mail, requests for autographs, and pianists seeking lessons, advice, or endorsements. A smaller percentage includes personal and professional correspondence between Vladimir Horowitz and notable figures, such as Claudio Arrau, Vladimir Ashkenazy, Gina Bachauer, Leonard Bernstein, Stephen Bishop, Martin Bookspan, Dave Brubeck, Van Cliburn, Leon Fleisher, Nelson Freire, Gary Graffman (filed under Wilfrid Van Wyck), André Gregory, W. Averell Harriman, Byron Janis, Erich Leinsdorf, Goddard Lieberson, Eugene List, Bess Myerson, Luther Noss, Guiomar Novaes, Leontyne Price (filed under Hubert Dilworth), Tony Randall, Arthur Rubinstein, David Sarnoff, Rudolf Serkin, Ruth Slencynska, Risë Stevens, Alexandre Tansman, Rosalyn Tureck, and Ronald Turini.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of compositions by Vladimir Horowitz - or thought to be by Horowitz. Most are undated. Except as otherwise noted, the compositions are for piano.

did.physdesc: Autograph manuscript, signed, complete, written in black ink, folio, double staff, 4 pages. Edges chipped, with some loss of notes and part of signature.
Computer-generated printout of edition by Ronn Yedidia.
Autograph manuscript, signed, incomplete, folio, doubled staff, 1 page.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Begins with the first 9 measures of Fragment douloureux. Verso: sketch of vocal melody with Russian text, unsigned, incomplete, folio, double staff, 9 measures.

did.physdesc: Autograph manuscript, signed, incomplete, folio, 8 pages.
Autograph manuscript, unsigned, incompletely-written in red ink, folio, double staff, 2 pages.
Autograph manuscript, signed, incomplete, written in red ink, folio, double staff, 3 1/2 pages.
Autograph manuscript, signed, incomplete, written in black ink, folio, double staff, 4 pages.

did.unittitle: Fragment douloureux, Un conte, Tableau musical "Tempo Valse".
did.physdesc: Autograph manuscript, unsigned, incomplete, written in black ink, folio, double staff, 2 1/2 pages.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Edited by Ronn Yedidia

did.physdesc: Autograph manuscript, unsigned, incomplete, written in black ink, folio, double staff, 1 leaf with 8 measures on recto.
Autograph manuscript, signed, incomplete, written in black ink, folio, single staff (18 staves per page), 1 1/2 pages.
Autograph manuscript, signed, incomplete, written in black ink, folio, double staff, 2 pages.

did.unittitle: Variationen [on Paganini's Caprice XXIV. after Kreisler], Tempo Valse.
did.physdesc: Autograph manuscript, signed. Incomplete, written in black ink, folio, triple staff, 2 pages.
Autograph manuscript, signed, complete, triple staff, 3 pages.
autograph manuscript, signed [part of signature chipped off], incomplete, written in black ink, folio, double staff, 4 1/2 pages.
Autograph manuscript, signed, incomplete, folio, double staff, 2 pages.
Unsigned, incomplete, folio, double staff, 2 pages. "First compostion VH" written in another hand.
Unsigned and incomplete. Folio triple staff, 1 page. For violin and piano, verso 35 mm. of chord progressions with roman numerals underneath.
Autograph manuscript, unsigned, incomplete, folio, double staff, 7 measures on 1 leaf. "marcatissimo e staccatissimo" written below first staff.
autograph manuscript, unsigned, incomplete, folio, double and triple staff, 7 measures on 1 leaf. Staves 7-9 for piano and melody instrument, D-major. ; quarto.
Autograph manuscript, signed, complete, quarto, double staff, 2 pages.
Autograph manuscript, signed, incomplete, quarto double staff, 5 pages.
Autograph manuscript, signed, incomplete, quarto, double staff, 12 pages (first four pages are photocopies). Recto: unidentified sketches, 13 measures.
Autograph manuscript, unsigned, incomplete, quarto, double staff, 1 leaf. 18 measures of sketches. Verso: 7 measures of music
Autograph manuscript, unsigned, incomplete, double staff, 1 leaf. 8 measures, D major, marked "Fine" at end.
Autograph manuscript, signed "Vladimir Gorovich". Complete, 4to, double staff, 4 pages. For piano.
Unidentified sketches, 3 leaves.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Comprised of music, printed and manuscript, sent to Vladimir Horowitz by various amateur composers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of general photographs, portraits, inscribed photos, albums and slides. General photographs feature Vladimir Horowitz, Wanda Toscanini Horowitz, Sonia Horowitz and friends.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographer: Grand Uff. V. Laviosa

did.unittitle: Photographs of Vladimir Horowitz and Wanda Toscanini Horowitz
Oversize photo of VH and WTH in their apartment, with dog
Oversize portrait of Vladimir Horowitz and Wanda Toscanini Horowitz
Two oversize, candid photos of VH and WTH walking in the street
Photographs (head shots) of Wanda Toscanini Horowitz
Photo album: Wanda Toscanini Horowitz, Arturo Toscanini, Wally Toscanini, family and friends
Photo album: Sonia Horowitz, alone and with family
Photo album: Wanda Toscanini Horowitz, alone and with friends
Photo album: Wanda Toscanini Horowitz, alone and with friends
Photo album: Vladimir Horowitz, Wanda Toscanini Horowitz, and Arturo Toscanini
Souvenir album from the White House

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This album contains photographs of VH and WTH visiting President Jimmy Carter in the White House.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This box contains seven small containers of color slides. Six contain portraits of Vladimir Horowitz, and the seventh contains shots of Sonia Horowitz circa 1965.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vladimir Horowitz rehearsal for recital at Yale University

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vladimir Horowitz rehearsal for Yale recital, cameras B & C

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vladimir Horowitz rehearsal for Yale recital, camera A

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This box contains clippings, materials related to the National Horowitz Steinway Piano Tour, press releases and advertisements, materials related to AT, artwork, magazines featuring the Toscanini Horowitz family, and WTH's passports and funeral prayer cards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Large St. Sikora Medal in Green Box (1849); Academia Nationale S. Cecilia Medal (1966); Tzar Petros Alexiewitz Medal (1953); Jesse H. Jones Hall for the Performing Arts, Houston, TX Medal (1966); Arturo Toscanini Gold Centennial Medal (1967); Arturo Toscanini Silver Centennial Medal (1967); Large Franz Liszt Medal in Red Box (1978); Grammy Nominee Medal (1992); Association Des Anciens Eleves du Conservatoire de Paris inscribed to Vladimir Horowitz (No Date); Franz Liszt Medal by F. Stiasny (No Date); Franz Liszt Medal set in red velvet frame (No Date); F. Liszt framed silhouette (No Date); F. Liszt Bronze Medal (No Date)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "To Maestro Arturo Toscanini, In commemoration of the 20th Anniversary of the Israel Philharmonic Orchestra and your inspring leadership at its first concert. With Grateful Apprecition, American Fund for Israel Institutions, Fredric R. Mann, Chairman of the Board, Samuel Rubin, President, 1936-1956"

did.unittitle: Framed sketch of Franz Liszt, with dedication on back by Franz Liszt

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Best Classical Permofmance, Instrumental Soloist (without orchestra) 1992: Vladimir Horowitz - Discovered Treasures"

did.unittitle: Wax mold of V. Horowitz's hands
Plaster mold of V. Horowitz's hands
Bronze sculpture of V. Horowitz's hands
Bow tie and award ribbons w/ pin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Six decorations awarded to Vladimir Horowitz: Presidential Medal of Freedom awarded to V.H. by President Ronald Reagan (1986), Cavaliere di Gran Croce della Republica Italiana awarded by President Cossiga (1986), Ordre National de La Legion D'Honneur awarded by President Francois Mitterand (1985), Medaglia d'Oro conferred on Vladimir Horowitz by Il Comune de Milano presented by the Mayor of Milan (1985), German medal awarded to V.H. by the Senator of Hamburg (2)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four decorative spoons

did.unittitle: Framed photo of Chopin with gold stars
Framed picture, "1961 - A mon tres cher Volodja, ce souvenir de Valdemosa [sic], Wally"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an artist's print of Vladimir Horowitz, a page from a 15th-century Book of Hours, and various clippings and photographs of artwork.

did.unittitle: Ordre National De La Legion D'Honneur presented to Vladimir Horowitz
Cavaliere di Gran Croce award, presented to Vladimir Horowitz
"A Steinway will never sound quite the same again" plaque
Proclamation of Vladimir Horowitz Week, Office of the Governor, State of Missouri
Rare material collected by Vladimir Horowitz

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rare historical items collected by Vladimir Horowitz include the following: Letter from S. Rachmaninoff to Paul Hugo Litwinsky (1937); signed photo of Giuseppe Verdi (1890); signed photo of Franz Liszt (no date); signed photo of Ignacy Paderewski; photocopy of letter to Arturo Toscanini from Albert Einstein (1936); note signed by Hector Berlioz; letter signed by Vincenzo Bellini; five letters of Giacomo Rossini; letter and composition by Anton Diabelli; music manuscript possibly by Richard Wagner; manuscript excerpt from "Aida" by Giuseppe Verdi (1873); letter from Hans von Bulow; letter from Giovanni Battista Rubini.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes two sets of index cards, describing concert "conditions" (weather, number of attendees), hotel receipts, and concert riders for Vladimir Horowitz.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Programs and press releases related to the tour of Vladimir Horowitz's Steinway Piano. Includes a plaque reading, "This concert grand piano was Mr. Horowitz' favorite piano. He played the last notes on this piano the night before he died."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copyright certificate for Vladimir Horowitz's "Concert Fantasy"; baptism document, poem, and funeral cards for Sonia Horowitz; "List for Mr. Horowitz's Golden Jubilee at the Steinway Piano"; "Inventory of contents" for the Horowitz residence in New Milford, CT; and two U.S. passports for Wanda Toscanini Horowitz.

did.unittitle: Clippings, magazines, press releases and advertisements

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Related to performances and recordings by Vladimir Horowitz.

did.unittitle: Oversize poster, "Vladimir Horowitz with the New York Philharmonic"
"Carnaval (Schumann) Wladimir Horowitz zum andenken an sein Erstes Auftreten in Berlin" publicity

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a photograph of Giacomo Puccini, with a dedication to Carla Toscanini; a Russian caricature; clippings; Toscanini commemorative envelopes; photocopy of a 1921 article from Il Popolo with annotations; and a publication by Stefan Zweig.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings and magazines related to Vladimir Horowitz and Wanda Toscanini Horowitz.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists chiefly of fan mail to Vladimir Horowitz.

did.unittitle: Press books and advertisements for Vladimir Horowitz

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains programs, press releases, and publicity photos. Also includes a photograph of Toscanini in Italy, dated Feb. 26, 1900.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains clippings from magazines and newspapers. Includes a full issue of Life magazine, 1939, which features "Toscanini and granddaughter" on the cover.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes tickets to Vladimir Horowitz concerts, receipts, and a program for a 1966 Russian concert of Sergei Rachmaninoff.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: John Fiske Robson was an elementary school music teacher in Pelham, New York, and a vehement fan of Vladimir Horowitz. Robson donated letters, clippings and photographs to the Gilmore Music Library, in honor of Horowitz. It was decided that Robson's materials should be kept with the Horowitz Papers as a prime example of the dedication of Horowitz's most loyal fans. Note that there is some similar material in Series IX. of the Horowitz Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The majority of the correspondence is from Paul Robson to Vladimir Horowitz and Wanda Toscanini Horowitz. Only a couple of Robson's letters appear to have been answered. Notable is a letter describing John Robson's gift to the Horowitz archive.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder contains a variety of photographs, both black and white and color, as well as 3 negatives. Subjects include posed photos, snapshots, and one photograph with a dedication to Paul Robson.

did.unittitle: Oversize portrait of Vladimir Horowitz sitting on couch with cigarette

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Most of the clippings are from the New York Times, and date from the 1960s, 1970s, and 1980s. Many of them detail Horowitz's "comeback" concert in 1965. Also included is a brief addition to Wanda Toscanini Horowitz's obituary (1998) from "Lesbian and Gay New York", that discusses Horowitz's identity as a gay man.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder includes programs, concert tickets, "line position tickets", and lists of concert-goers in order of line position. Also included is an invitation from the "All Night Queue Committee" to an afternoon tea with Vladimir and Wanda Horowitz, with a newspaper clipping detailing the event.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cassette tape made by Robson for Vladimir Horowitz's 75th birthday. Mostly dubbed recordings, with a letter to Horowitz read by Robson (side 2).
